campus: Milan (joint inter-university degree with the University of Pavia; Milano-Bicocca is the administrative headquarters)
degree_class: L-30 (Degrees in Physical Sciences and Technologies)
admission_type: restricted access (limited enrolment with entry-requirements examination via CISIA test; ranking-based selection)

academic_requirements.summary: Limited-enrolment (number-capped) inter-university bachelor's programme delivered jointly with the University of Pavia, taught entirely in English in blended mode (180 ECTS over 3 years). Admission is by ranking based on a mandatory CISIA test, plus a B2 English requirement.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: An upper-secondary-school diploma granting access to university, or an equivalent foreign qualification recognised as suitable (valid if obtained after at least 12 years of schooling and granting university access in the issuing country; any required academic-aptitude certification of the issuing country is also needed).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Candidates must take a CISIA admissions test - for Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies this is CEnT-S, or TOLC-S, or the English TOLC-I. Admission is by ranking of test results against available places (the call does not publish a fixed minimum cut-off score for this programme; ranking is per selection criteria).
academic_requirements.available_places: 98 places for EU/equivalent and Marco Polo candidates for A.Y. 2026/2027 (20 first selection + 38 second selection + 40 third selection, of which 3 reserved for Marco Polo candidates). For A.Y. 2025/2026 a total of 120 places were available, plus 50 places reserved for early admission to 2026/2027.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Foreign-qualification candidates apply and upload their documents on apply.unimib.it; non-EU candidates requiring a visa take part in a dedicated selection reserved for them.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 or higher (CEFR). The B2 requirement applies specifically to Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies among the Science-area programmes in this call.
accepted_proofs: An English language certificate recognised by the University corresponding to level B2 or higher, obtained no more than 5 calendar years before the application deadline.
Holding a passport from a recognised English-speaking country (e.g. Australia, Canada, Ireland, Malta, New Zealand, United Kingdom, USA, and others listed in the call).

Candidates with an Italian qualification who do not hold a certificate may sit an in-person English language proficiency test at the University of Milano-Bicocca on set dates (1st session 23-30 March 2026; 2nd session 22 June-3 July 2026; 3rd session 2-7 September 2026).
language_requirements.certificate_upload_or_deadline: Candidates must upload the B2 certificate (among those recognised by the University) on Segreterie OnLine / apply.unimib.it; the requirement must be met by the application deadline of the relevant selection (foreign-qualification candidates may be admitted subject to obtaining certification by 31 July 2026).
language_requirements.notes: B2 English is explicitly required and verified during the application/enrolment procedure.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: CISIA admissions test - CEnT-S, or TOLC-S, or English TOLC-I (any of these is accepted for Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies).
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants to the bachelor (EU/equivalent, non-EU visa applicants and Marco Polo candidates).
entry_exam_or_test.format: Taken via the CISIA platform (cisiaonline.it) at any CISIA member university and in any mode (TOLC@UNI / CEnT@UNI or TOLC@CASA / CEnT@CASA); not required to be taken at Milano-Bicocca. The English TOLC-I is the English-language engineering/science test (the programme page links the English TOLC-I booking).
entry_exam_or_test.dates: The test must be taken from 1 January 2024 and by the deadline set for each 2026/2027 selection session.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] Admission is by ranking of test scores against available places; the 2026/2027 EU call refers to minimum scores in the 'Criteri di selezione' (selection criteria) section but a single fixed programme cut-off is not separately stated here.

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027 (EU citizens, equivalent and Marco Polo - application on Segreterie OnLine / apply.unimib.it): 1st session 18 February 2026 - 31 March 2026; 2nd session 15 April 2026 - 6 July 2026; 3rd session 7 July 2026 - 7 September 2026.

The CISIA test must have been taken from 1 January 2024 and by the deadline of the chosen session; candidates enrolled in the final year of secondary school may take part in the first and second selections.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrolment and payment of the first instalment follow the ranking publication for each session; foreign-qualification candidates accept the offer and submit enrolment documentation within the deadlines set per session (with the B2 certificate obtainable by 31 July 2026 for those admitted subject to it).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Dates above are taken directly from the 2026/2027 EU/equivalent call PDF (PROT_Bando LT SCIENZE 26-27 COMUNITARI). They align with the institution-wide apply.unimib.it 2026/27 EU calendar.

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027 (apply.unimib.it admission calls for non-EU citizens residing abroad and requiring a study visa - bachelor's): 1st call 7 November 2025 - 16 January 2026; 2nd call 16 March 2026 - 16 April 2026.

Reference structure from the 2025/2026 Physical Sciences English call (non-EU visa selection): English TOLC-I taken from February 2024 and by 18 April 2025; application for inclusion in the ranking on apply.unimib.it by 2 May 2025; ranking published, acceptance of offer and deposit by 12 May 2025; registration on Universitaly for the entry (type-D study) visa; closing of the ranking 15 July 2025.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Per the 2025/2026 non-EU procedure: pre-enrolment via Universitaly under 'International Students'; English B2 certificate by 31 July 2025; enrolment documents by 31 October 2025; study visa issued by 31 January 2026; on-site check of original documents by 28 February 2026. For 2026/2027 the entry visa must be issued by 31 January 2027 and originals checked by 28 February 2027.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants take part only in the dedicated non-EU selection on apply.unimib.it. The 2026/2027 EU call PDF covers EU/equivalent/Marco Polo candidates; the non-EU programme-specific dates here are drawn from the latest published non-EU Physical Sciences call (2025/2026) plus the 2026/27 institution-wide non-EU calendar.

required_documents.document_name: Upper-secondary-school diploma (or certificate of attendance / provisional certificate if not yet graduated)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on apply.unimib.it (foreign-qualification candidates) for verification of the foreign qualification before the application deadline.

required_documents.document_name: Evidence of 12-year school record (or 1-2 years of academic studies / foundation course) and any required country entrance-examination certificate
required_documents.notes: Required where the foreign qualification is awarded after fewer than 12 years of schooling or where the issuing country requires an academic-aptitude test.

required_documents.document_name: Identity document (passport for non-EU; ID card for EU or EU-equated applicants)
required_documents.notes: Passport required for non-EU candidates; ID card accepted for EU/equated candidates.

required_documents.document_name: CISIA test result (English TOLC-I / TOLC-S / CEnT-S)
required_documents.notes: Mandatory for inclusion in the selection ranking; obtained via the CISIA portal.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate at B2 level (among those recognised by the University)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on Segreterie OnLine / apply.unimib.it; certificate must be no older than 5 years. Candidates without a certificate may sit the UNIMIB in-person English test or use a passport-based exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment receipt, entry study visa and residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Non-EU visa applicants register on Universitaly under 'International Students'; the entry visa must be issued by the published deadline (31 January 2026 for 2025/26; 31 January 2027 for 2026/27).

source_quotes.quote: All the lectures are delivered in English and integrate traditional in-person teaching with dedicated online activities.
CEnT-S o TOLC-S o English TOLC-I (per Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies)
Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies 20 38 40 (di cui n. 3 posti destinati ai candidati del Programma Marco Polo) 98
1° sessione: dal 18 febbraio 2026 al 31 marzo 2026; 2° sessione: dal 15 aprile 2026 al 6 luglio 2026; 3° sessione: dal 7 luglio 2026 al 7 settembre 2026
6. Requisito di conoscenza della lingua inglese di livello pari o superiore al B2 (solo per Physical Sciences for Innovative Technologies)

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language: CONFIRMED fully English (joint inter-university degree with the University of Pavia, delivered in blended mode, 180 ECTS).
B2 English: CONFIRMED required (B2 or higher; certificate, passport exemption, or in-person UNIMIB English test).

Entry test: CONFIRMED a mandatory CISIA test - CEnT-S, TOLC-S, or English TOLC-I. A single fixed numerical cut-off for this programme is not separately published; selection is by ranking, so entry_exam_or_test scoring is marked uncertain.

EU/non-EU calls: CONFIRMED separate procedures. The 2026/2027 EU/equivalent call PDF gives exact EU session windows (18 Feb-31 Mar; 15 Apr-6 Jul; 7 Jul-7 Sep 2026). The 2026/2027 non-EU programme-specific dates are drawn from the latest published non-EU Physical Sciences call (2025/2026) combined with the apply.unimib.it 2026/27 non-EU calendar and should be reconfirmed from the 2026/2027 non-EU/English Physical Sciences call PDF once published.
tuition_or_fees_link points to the general UNIMIB fees-and-funding page; no programme-specific fee amount is invented.
